codeReview.summary: Trusted rule loading and allowlist enforcement were implemented, but project-policy context limits are not preserved, so effective rules can silently widen review scope relative to policy.

issues.description: mergeEffectivePolicy seeds ContextLinesBefore/After and MaxChangedLines/MaxFiles from SettingsFromPolicy(project) before checking whether project is nil. For repositories with no project policy, SettingsFromPolicy(nil) returns global defaults (20/20/2500/80), so the loader overwrites any platform-specified limits with unrelated context-package defaults. The same bug also drops platform-provided extra-based limits whenever a project policy exists but does not override them. This violates the required platform-defaults/project-policy merge behavior and can expand outbound review scope beyond configured trusted defaults.

sharedStateObservations.observation: PlatformDefaults exposes Extra plus rule-scope limit fields, but the current rules-loader implementation does not document how platform-level context/max-file defaults should map into EffectivePolicy. The worker implemented merge logic that fell back to internal/context package defaults instead of preserving platform values, suggesting this precedence rule is not captured clearly in shared guidance.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: internal/rules/loader.go:157-160 fills EffectivePolicy context/max limits from SettingsFromPolicy(project); internal/context/assembler.go:158-161 defines those as generic defaults 20/20/2500/80; no corresponding note appears in AGENTS.md or .factory/library/integrations.md.

summary: Review failed: the feature adds trusted REVIEW.md loading and prompt-injection isolation, but mergeEffectivePolicy incorrectly replaces platform-configured context/max-file limits with generic assembler defaults, breaking policy precedence and potentially widening review scope.
